rule: If a pedestrian is within 5 meters, the vehicle must stop immediately regardless of speed.
In rainy weather, stopping distance increases by 40% due to reduced friction.
If time-to-collision is less than 2 seconds, immediate braking is required.
In foggy conditions, speed must be reduced by at least 30% and following distance doubled.
A vehicle traveling at 50 km/h requires at least 14 meters to stop under normal conditions.
In snowy or icy conditions, stopping distance increases by up to 300%.
If a car is detected within 10 meters and ego speed exceeds 40 km/h, initiate braking.
When risk score exceeds 1.0, the vehicle must prioritize stopping over all other actions.
A pedestrian crossing or standing within the vehicle's path always has right of way.
If multiple objects are detected, prioritize the one with the lowest time-to-collision.
At speeds above 60 km/h, minimum safe following distance is 30 meters.
In clear weather at 30 km/h, stopping distance is approximately 8 meters.
A child or pedestrian detected near a crosswalk requires speed reduction to below 20 km/h.
If ego speed is 0, no collision risk exists regardless of object distance.
When a vehicle ahead brakes suddenly, time-to-collision drops rapidly and requires immediate response.
Reduced visibility in fog or rain requires the vehicle to operate in a more conservative decision mode.
If risk score is between 0.5 and 1.0, the vehicle should slow down and prepare to stop.
Night driving requires 20% longer stopping distance due to reduced reaction time.
Any object within the stopping distance threshold must trigger a STOP decision.
When LLM confidence is below 0.7, the rule engine decision should take priority.
A vehicle approaching an intersection must reduce speed to below 30 km/h.
Emergency vehicles with sirens require all nearby vehicles to pull over and stop.
In wet weather, minimum safe distance to any pedestrian doubles to 10 meters.
If time-to-collision is between 2 and 4 seconds, slow down and monitor closely.
At highway speeds above 80 km/h, lane changes require at least 50 meters of clear space.
